raga.name: Malkauns
raga.description: A pentatonic raga of immense depth and gravity. Omits Re and Pa entirely, creating a haunting, gapped scale. Associated with the deepest hours of night. Said to have the power to invoke supernatural concentration. One of the most powerful ragas in the system.

forbidden.name: Rishabh (any form)
forbidden.reason: Malkauns is an audav (pentatonic) raga — Re is completely absent. Its inclusion would destroy the characteristic gaps.

forbidden.name: Pancham
forbidden.reason: Pa is completely absent from Malkauns. This omission is what gives the raga its unusual, otherworldly quality — the most stable interval after Sa is deliberately removed.

movement.aroha: S g m d n S'
movement.avaroha: S' n d m g S
pakad: g m g S n S
movement.pakad_string: m d n d, m g S, g m g S n S

characteristic_phrases.context: Opening — oscillation around Sa through komal Ga

characteristic_phrases.phrase: m d n d m g
characteristic_phrases.context: The definitive Malkauns phrase — ascent and immediate descent around komal Dha

characteristic_phrases.phrase: g m g S n S
characteristic_phrases.context: Resolution phrase — the low Ni approaching Sa from below is haunting
Upper register — brief touch of upper Sa before retreating
Full descent — the pentatonic steps create a meditative gravity

note_rules.Sa_approach: Approached from komal Ga above (g S) or komal Ni below (n S) — both create characteristic Malkauns tension
note_rules.Ma_treatment: The most important note — acts as the gravitational centre since Pa is absent
note_rules.absence_of_Pa: The deliberate omission of Pa removes the strongest consonance after the octave, making every phrase feel suspended and searching
note_rules.pentatonic_character: The wide gaps between notes (minor 3rds) give Malkauns its spacious, meditative quality

vadi.role: King note — Ma becomes the anchor since Pa is absent. Receives the deepest, most sustained treatment.

samvadi.role: Queen note — Sa serves as the resolution point. The Ma→Sa axis is the emotional spine of Malkauns.

context.rasa: Veera (heroic) and Shanta (peace) — the stillness of deep concentration
context.aesthetic: Evokes the absolute stillness of deep night — the hour when the world sleeps and the mind turns inward. The pentatonic gaps create a sense of vast, empty space.

western_equivalent.key_example: C minor pentatonic (C Eb F Ab Bb)
western_equivalent.relationship: Closely related to the minor pentatonic used universally in blues, rock, and folk music worldwide. The specific pattern (1 b3 4 b6 b7) is the same as the Japanese Yo scale and appears in many world music traditions.
common_pentatonic_songs: Pentatonic blues licks across all guitar traditions

lofi.notes: Minor pentatonic is the backbone of hip-hop melody. Malkauns maps perfectly to lofi's introspective mood. The absence of Pa creates a haunting, unresolved quality ideal for late-night beats.

ambient.notes: Perhaps the single most ambient-compatible raga. The pentatonic spaciousness, the meditative mood, the deep-night association — all align perfectly with ambient aesthetics.

calm.notes: Deeply calm, but with an intensity that goes beyond mere relaxation. Works for meditation and deep focus tracks.

upbeat.notes: Malkauns is fundamentally a raga of stillness and depth. Upbeat treatment would fight against its nature.

jazz_fusion.notes: Pentatonic minor variant offers spacious modal freedom.

cinematic.notes: Haunting meditative quality for profound scenes.

downtempo.notes: Wide note gaps create natural spaciousness.

electronic.notes: Proven in disco; pentatonic phrases loop cleanly on synths.

chillhop.notes: Meditative lines pair beautifully with jazzy swing.

reggae_dub.notes: Strong fit: minor pentatonic lines sit naturally over heavy sub-bass; dub delay complements the spacious phrasing.

trap.notes: Great for catchy minor hooks; pentatonic contour makes motifs easy to remember and replay.

rock.notes: Works well for riff-based rock/psych; pentatonic shapes map naturally onto guitar.

fusion_notes: Pentatonic (no Re, no Pa) creates wide gaps ideal for ambient pads and electronic layering
Because Re and Pa are absent, avoid dominant-function basslines (V–I gestures). Use drones/pedals and modal bass motion.
Spacing is part of the aesthetic: leave silence between phrases so the pentatonic 'voids' feel intentional.
If you add harmony, keep it minimal (drones, power-chords, or slow-moving clusters) so the raga does not collapse into generic minor pentatonic.
